title: Who Bids Together, Wins Together
subtitle: When the same vendors repeatedly appear in the same competitive tenders — and take turns winning — it stops looking like competition and starts looking like coordination.

stats.totalPairsSub: unique vendor pairs tracked across all sectors
stats.flaggedPairsSub: co-bid rate above 50% with 10+ shared procedures
stats.sharedProceduresSub: total co-bidding events among flagged pairs

methodology.title: How RUBLI detects bid rigging
methodology.body: We analyze every competitive tender in COMPRANET to find vendor pairs that bid on the same procedures at abnormally high rates. A co-bid rate above 50% with 10+ shared procedures suggests coordination — especially when combined with low win-rate variance (bid rotation) or one vendor consistently losing (cover bidding). These patterns are invisible in individual contracts but emerge clearly at scale.

patterns.title: Collusion Patterns
patterns.bidRotationDesc: Vendors take turns winning — A wins round 1, B wins round 2. Win rates are nearly equal.
patterns.coverBiddingDesc: One vendor always loses, submitting deliberately high bids to create the appearance of competition.
patterns.marketAllocationDesc: Vendors divide contracts by geography, institution, or time period — never competing in each other's territory.
patterns.unknownDesc: Pattern detected but not yet classified — requires further investigation.

tooltip.coBidRateBody: Co-bid rate is the percentage of procedures where both vendors submitted bids simultaneously. Rates above 70% combined with high shared procedure volume may indicate coordinated bidding — where vendors collude to influence which one wins.

empty.title: No pairs match current filters
empty.body: Try lowering the minimum shared procedures threshold or disabling the flagged-only filter. Co-bidding pairs with fewer than 10 shared procedures are excluded by default because isolated co-bidding is normal market behavior.

error.title: COMPRANET co-bidding data unavailable
error.body: The collusion analysis endpoint returned an error. This may indicate the co_bidding_stats table needs to be rebuilt. Try again or check backend logs.
